2019-12-01.title: Leonardo DiCaprio Responds to Brazil’s President About Amazon Fires
2019-12-01.abstract: The actor and environmentalist released a statement on Saturday after President Jair Bolsonaro falsely accused him of funding the fires recently set in the Amazon rainforest.

2019-12-01.title: China Cracks Down on Fentanyl. But Is It Enough to End the U.S. Epidemic?
2019-12-01.abstract: After years of American pressure, China is taking steps to shut off the illicit supply of deadly synthetic opioids. But don’t expect an end to the overdoses.

2019-12-01.title: Hong Kong Police Fire Tear Gas as Protesters Return in Force
2019-12-01.abstract: After a week of relative calm and a vote that boosted protesters’ cause, officers fired tear gas and rubber bullets, and demonstrators attacked shops.

2019-12-01.title: ‘Sent Candy’: Cockpit Tapes Show Russian Pilots Bombing Syrian Civilians
2019-12-01.abstract: A Times investigation used cockpit recordings to show for the first time how Russian pilots attacked civilians in Syria this summer, killing dozens.

2019-12-01.title: ‘I Wanted to Die:’ Northern Ireland Confronts a Suicide Crisis
2019-12-01.abstract: Haunted by a violent past, the territory has one of the highest suicide rates in the world.

2019-12-02.title: Malta Leader Says He Will Resign, as Murder Inquiry Widens
2019-12-02.abstract: An investigation into the 2017 murder of Daphne Caruana Galizia has reached into the government, triggering protests and a promise by Prime Minister Joseph Muscat to quit next year.

2019-12-02.abstract: A short meeting of the alliance’s leaders in London is meant to avoid conflict, but there are worries about the intentions of Presidents Trump and Emmanuel Macron.

2019-12-02.abstract: For braving life inside the Demilitarized Zone, the 188 residents of a South Korean village get great perks, like tax breaks, exemption from military service and superfast internet access.

2019-12-03.abstract: A Maori man attacked a statue to raise awareness of his ancestors’ pain. A newspaper covered the story, and a very important reader took action: Prime Minister Jacinda Ardern.

2019-12-03.abstract: Manila’s airport was shut for 12 hours and half a million people were evacuated as the typhoon swept through the archipelago.

2019-12-03.abstract: President Trump said a warning from President Emmanuel Macron of France that Europe could no longer assume American support was “a very dangerous statement.” Mr. Macron said he stood by it.

2019-12-04.title: Cha In-ha Becomes Third Young Korean Entertainer to Die in Two Months

2019-12-04.abstract: The alliance’s 70th-anniversary gathering was punctuated by testy exchanges with President Trump and a video of world leaders appearing to criticize him.

2019-12-05.abstract: This is politics in a climate of fear and intimidation: Inconspicuous offices with no campaign posters and multiple security cameras. No canvassing after dark. Death threats.

2019-12-08.abstract: Throngs of people poured into major thoroughfares for a march marking Human Rights Day.

2019-12-21.abstract: After three months of demonstrations, the politicians and the protesters are talking past each other, there’s no prime minister and Iran maintains its influence.

2019-12-21.abstract: President Trump’s summits with Kim Jong-un have failed to bring concrete results, and the diplomatic vacuum has given North Korea more time to build its nuclear arsenal.

2019-12-25.abstract: Firefighters struggled to control the blaze on a hillside in Valparaíso. The city’s mayor said the fire might have been set intentionally.

2019-12-25.abstract: The scene, which Disney cut to preserve a PG-13 rating in the conservative nation, was the first overt appearance of gay characters in the “Star Wars” franchise.

2019-12-25.title: Japanese Lawmaker Is Arrested in Casino Bribery Investigation
2019-12-25.abstract: Tsukasa Akimoto, a member of the governing party, is suspected of accepting cash from a company that wanted to build a casino on the island of Hokkaido.

2019-12-26.title: He Vowed to Revitalize Italy’s Schools. After a Budget Loss, He’s Out.
2019-12-26.abstract: Education Minister Lorenzo Fioramonti promised to raise performance and introduce lessons on climate change, but Parliament’s budget left him a billion euros short.

2019-12-26.title: A Country Where Driving Makes You a Target
2019-12-26.abstract: Afghanistan’s highways are prime killing fields, with travelers dying in roadside bombings, ambushes and airstrikes.

2019-12-26.title: Christmas Typhoon Batters Central Philippines
2019-12-26.abstract: Thousands traveling home for the holiday were stranded as flights and ferries were canceled. At least 16 deaths were reported.

2019-12-26.title: Nicaragua Has a Simple Message for Protesters: Don’t
2019-12-26.abstract: For nearly two years, Nicaraguans have been rising up against their government. It has cost some dearly.

2019-12-26.title: Netanyahu’s Survival Is Tested as Likud Party Holds Leadership Vote
2019-12-26.abstract: Israel’s prime minister declares victory over a challenger from within his own party. An exit poll gives him a wide lead.

2019-12-27.title: Kazakhstan Plane Crash Kills at Least 12 People
2019-12-27.abstract: The crash in Almaty, the latest to befall a former Soviet republic, highlighted the region’s checkered air safety history. Dozens of passengers were injured, but others survived unharmed.

2019-12-27.title: Japan to Deploy Destroyer to Middle East
2019-12-27.abstract: The rare overseas mission follows an attack on a Japanese oil tanker and increased American pressure to play a more active role in the region.

2019-12-27.title: An Old Menstruation Taboo Killed Her. This Time, a Man Went to Jail.
2019-12-27.abstract: In a first for Nepal, the authorities have arrested a man who forced his sister-in-law to sleep out in a cold hut during her period.

2019-12-27.title: Blocked in U.S., Huawei Touts ‘Shared Values’ to Compete in Europe
2019-12-27.abstract: The Chinese tech giant is spending millions of dollars on a charm offensive in Brussels, hoping for a leading role in building 5G networks. It seems to be working.

2019-12-27.title: Tourists Drowned at Pool in Spain Because They Couldn’t Swim, Police Say
2019-12-27.abstract: A father and two children, ages 9 and 16, who died in a pool at a resort on the Costa del Sol, found themselves in deep water and unable to get out, the authorities said.

2019-12-28.title: ISIS Affiliate in Nigeria Releases a Video Showing 11 Executions
2019-12-28.abstract: The grisly footage shows fighters aligned with the Islamic State cutting the throats of 10 prisoners and shooting an 11th captive.

2019-12-28.title: Somalia Bombing Kills Nearly 80, Raising Fears of Resurgent Militancy
2019-12-28.abstract: A truck filled with explosives blew up at a busy intersection in the capital. It was the latest, and the worst, in a series of recent attacks.

2019-12-28.title: Norwegian Spy Jailed by Russia Is Free. He’s Angry, Too, but Not at Moscow.
2019-12-28.abstract: Frode Berg, a trusting pensioner, willingly worked for Norwegian intelligence. Then, he says, they hung him out to dry.

2019-12-28.title: In China’s Crackdown on Muslims, Children Have Not Been Spared
2019-12-28.abstract: Nearly half a million children, many of them ethnic minorities, have been placed in boarding schools in Xinjiang where the authorities aim to instill loyalty to China and the Communist Party.

2019-12-28.title: ‘The Pendulum Has Swung Back’: Latin America’s Corruption Fight Stalls
2019-12-28.abstract: A drive against graft that started in Brazil left many hoping for a fairer, more equal region. That era is over.

2019-12-29.title: North Korea Leader Urges ‘Offensive Measures’ at Top Party Meeting
2019-12-29.abstract: Analysts warn that Kim Jong-un may end a moratorium on testing long-range missiles and nuclear weapons.

2019-12-29.title: How a Chase Bank Chairman Helped the Deposed Shah of Iran Enter the U.S.
2019-12-29.abstract: The fateful decision in 1979 to admit Mohammed Reza Pahlavi prompted the seizure of the American Embassy in Tehran and helped doom the Carter presidency.

2019-12-29.title: Samoa Lifts State of Emergency After Deadly Measles Epidemic
2019-12-29.abstract: The Pacific island nation will reopen schools after a measles outbreak killed 81 people, many of them children.

2019-12-29.title: Voracious Appetite for Sea Cucumbers Fuels a Farm Boom, but at a Cost
2019-12-29.abstract: China’s demand for this unlovely marine animal, a relative of the starfish, has brought economic benefits to a region of the country famous for the delicacy. It has also depleted natural stocks.

2019-12-29.title: The U.N. Tried to Save Hospitals in Syria. It Didn’t Work.
2019-12-29.abstract: Russian and Syrian forces have bombed sites on a flawed U.N. no-strike list with impunity. Relief groups are giving up hope.

2019-12-30.title: Left Behind by Migrant Husbands, Women Break the Rules and Go to Work
2019-12-30.abstract: Wives are shocking their traditional West African villages by earning money and running large households while their husbands are in Europe seeking jobs.

2019-12-30.title: China Sentences Wang Yi, Christian Pastor, to 9 Years in Prison
2019-12-30.abstract: The founder of one of China’s largest unregistered churches was given a lengthy sentence for what the government called subversion of state power.

2019-12-30.title: In 2019, We Were There: 12 Favorite Dispatches From the Earth’s 4 Corners
2019-12-30.abstract: This year, we delivered 125 dispatches from 44 countries and six continents, each one offering an immersive experience of one of the world’s most noteworthy places. Here are the highlights.

2019-12-30.title: In Indonesia, Outlaw Gold Miners Poison Themselves to Survive
2019-12-30.abstract: One large mining company is trying to shut illegal operations, which use mercury. The small-scale miners say there’s no other way to earn a living.

2019-12-30.title: Woman Who Accused 12 Men of Rape Is Guilty of ‘Public Mischief’ in Cyprus
2019-12-30.abstract: The British woman said that a dozen Israeli men had raped her in a resort town, and then retracted her statement. She could face up to a year in prison.

2019-12-31.title: It’s 50 Below. The Past Is a Horror Show. You’d Dream of Escaping Too.
2019-12-31.abstract: Best known as the most feared and frigid outpost of the Soviet gulag, Magadan struggles to keep its residents from fleeing. The ice cream isn’t enough to keep them.

2019-12-31.title: Protesters Attack U.S. Embassy in Iraq, Chanting ‘Death to America’
2019-12-31.abstract: President Trump blamed Iran after demonstrators breached the compound’s outer wall. Tensions are high after American airstrikes killed members of an Iran-backed militia.

2019-12-31.title: Apocalyptic Scenes in Australia as Fires Turn Skies Blood Red
2019-12-31.abstract: Thousands of people fled from their homes as wildfires raged on the last day of the warmest decade on record in Australia.

2019-12-31.title: A Couple Unwittingly Threw Away $20,000. The Dump Gave It Back.
2019-12-31.abstract: The money, hidden in a box of possessions from a deceased relative, was returned thanks to a hawk-eyed (and honest) worker.

2019-12-31.title: French Chef Loses Legal Battle Over a Lost Michelin Star
2019-12-31.abstract: Marc Veyrat was outraged when La Maison des Bois, his restaurant in the French Alps, was stripped of the guide’s top rating.
